compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome has suffered a near-total collapse of visible biological specimens. The white rabbit anchor is missing from the current frame, and the primary plant specimens (P1-P4) are either absent or reduced to non-viable, necrotic remnants.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (Anchor missing, plant matter absent)

biome_observations.soil_texture: Desiccated, showing signs of extreme moisture deficit.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshell fragments in the black pot, likely an amateur attempt at calcium supplementation or pest deterrent, now serving no biological purpose.
biome_observations.surface_conditions: Desk surface is clear of organic matter; no signs of recent growth or maintenance.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Initial state showed a single leaf in the black pot and the rabbit anchor present.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Rapid degradation of the remaining leaf structure; total loss of the rabbit anchor; complete abandonment of the biome.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is non-viable. The plants have either been removed or have undergone complete senescence and decomposition.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue, absence of structural integrity in stems, and removal of the scale anchor.

anomalies: Removal of the white rabbit scale anchor.
Total disappearance of all registered plant life.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens transitioned from a state of minimal health to total disappearance. The removal of the scale anchor suggests a deliberate clearing of the desk space rather than natural decay alone. The environment is currently sterile and devoid of life.
